captured.evidence: brain is in the vessel-rich group which receives a large portion of cardiac output
wakes up quickly because the drug redistributes from the brain to muscle and fat, not because of hepatic metabolism
Context-sensitive half-time describes how the time for plasma levels to drop 50% changes depending on infusion duration

After a long infusion, the muscle and fat compartments are already saturated with drug, so there is no concentration gradient for redistribution anymore. Recovery then depends on the liver metabolizing the drug

missed.description: First-pass pulmonary uptake and its protective buffering effect
Impact of age and hypovolemia on central compartment size and dosing requirements

summary: You correctly explained redistribution vs. elimination and context-sensitive half-time concepts. Missing discussion of pulmonary uptake and age-related pharmacokinetic changes.
voss_quip: Solid grasp of compartment basics, but you missed the lungs entirely.
